title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast meets with Valerie Brand, the wife of Josea Brand. Valerie is described as having a pale complexion and a weak, melancholic voice, appearing to enjoy her state of illness. When Hakast shows her a photograph of the murder victim, an insurance agent, Valerie expresses curiosity but incorrectly assumes the man was strangled; Hakast clarifies that he was stabbed. Valerie claims she does not know the man and asks if he had any connection to their neighbor, Miss Pembroke, but Hakast states that Miss Pembroke did not know him.

Josea Brand explains that while he is not a gardener himself, he employs Mr. McNaughton from number 63 to maintain their garden. Regarding the timeline of the murder, which occurred between one and three o'clock, Josea states that he and Valerie were having lunch together around one or one-thirty. He notes that their dining room window faces the street, meaning they could not see the garden, and that Valerie typically rests upstairs after lunch. Josea also reveals that the couple's lifestyle improved a year ago after Valerie inherited money from an uncle she had not seen in twenty-five years. He expresses an interest in traveling to Greece, though Valerie prefers to remain in England due to her health and social connections.

After leaving the Brands, Hakast and Colin Lamb discuss the case. Meanwhile, at 62 Wilbraham Crescent, Mrs. Rymes deals with a commotion in her kitchen caused by her sons, Bill and Ted, who were using jars as bowling balls. The boys engage in a wrestling match, prompting Mrs. Rymes to push them out of the kitchen so she can clean up the broken ceramics.
